people_highlights.name: Sam Altman
people_highlights.post:
1. We believe in iterative deployment; although GPT-5.5 is already a smart model, we expect rapid improvements. Iterative deployment is a big part of our safety strategy; we believe the world will be best equipped to win at the team sport of AI resilience this way.

people_highlights.name: Ethan Mollick
people_highlights.post: We really need a better word for the good kind of AI psychosis, the one where someone goes into a fugue state with the latest model and returns 40 days later from the mountaintop with something new.

people_highlights.name: Google DeepMind
people_highlights.post:
For @DemisHassabis, the path to AGI started in 1988 with an Amiga 500 and a game of Othello. 🕹️

His epiphany that software could act on our behalf remains at the heart of our work today as we apply the same logic to solving scientific grand challenges.

Read more on @FastCompany

trending_posts.post:
🔸SHANTAE (7 Sirens) MODEL RELEASE  - UPDATE🔸

My Shantae model, released back in late 2021, has gotten a complete makeover: new textures, accessories and rig! 

Current owners can download the new files from their respective libaries.

👇 Link below👇

#b3d https://t.co/m6cM6iJdL8

It happened. Claude Code has been dethroned.

I've lived in Codex w/ ChatGPT 5.5 the last couple of days and it is the:

• Smartest model
• Best all around AI app
• Most powerful features (computer use and image gen)

Here's everything you need to know about this A+ release: http

Finally, the long project that I almost forgot got into beta release. You can try it by downloading the artifact from GitHub. https://t.co/w9IefZCXYe. Currently, it is only available with Gemini translation. The stable release will try to include the local LLM as a translator. ht
@br___ian I can't wait for my entire business model to be obsoleted by a release by a major LLM company

You know, it's painfully obvious that Microsoft has replaced a *large* chunk of their coders with poorly written and even more poorly managed LLM/AI models. 

I've used Office, specifically Word, since the late 90s, and every release generally was better than the last, but that

@asahdzerfas @IanMuir63542272 @asmanm85 What never makes the press release: every major LLM deployment has internal 'shadow evals' — benchmarks that don't get published because the failure modes are too embarrassing. The public benchmarks are curated for performance. The private
@aleabitoreddit with what compute are you going to release "custom llms" and there's no open source llm that you could create a lora on that would be better than vanilla claude
@stefanroberts We have solution to this tough UK limitation. Already launched preview release of a 100% UK LLM; called Lizzy, check it out: https://t.co/cG4BEi7BZo

@theagenticmind Smart move by OpenAI. Promptfoo was already the go-to for LLM eval and red teaming in most shops I've seen. Bringing that in-house means security testing gets baked into the model release cycle rather than bolted on after.
@om_patel5 same shit with every new model release. llm psychosis is getting worse
